notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix → /nw-deliver. The authoritative specification is the RCA at `docs/feature/fix-eval-reconciler-discarded/deliver/bugfix-rca.md` (user-validated; explicit approval of Option A — dispatch only the reconciler named in `eval.reconciler` via `reconcilers_iter().find(|r| r.name() == reconciler_name)` with log+skip on miss. Option B — keep the registry-iterating fan-out and document it — was REJECTED on grounds enumerated in the RCA: would require a new contract document explaining why the broker's key includes a field the dispatcher ignores, defensive target-shape handling in every reconciler that joins the registry, and a revised §8 invariant weaker than the whitepaper currently promises).

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: signature change + body rewrite + caller update + 5 cascade test updates land in ONE cohesive commit. No `#[deprecated]` aliases, no shadow function with feature flag, no two-phase rollout. Per the user feedback memory, removed is removed.

Two-step Outside-In TDD shape, mirroring the prior bugfix at `docs/feature/fix-noop-self-reenqueue/deliver/roadmap.json`. Step 01-01 writes the failing regression test (RED). The test EXTENDS the existing file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` rather than creating a new one (per RCA §Regression test design — "extend the existing file rather than create a new one"); the file is already registered through `tests/acceptance.rs`'s `mod acceptance { ... }` block from `fix-noop-self-reenqueue` and needs no further wiring. Test marked `#[ignore = "RED scaffold for fix-eval-reconciler-discarded — un-ignore in the GREEN step"]` so the lefthook nextest-affected pre-commit pass stays green without `--no-verify`. Step 01-02 lands the production fix, removes the `#[ignore]`, AND mechanically updates the 5 cascade test sites that today happen to compile because the registry-iterating fan-out makes the missing reconciler-name parameter irrelevant.

RED via runtime assertion failure (NOT compile failure). The new test exercises only existing public surface — `AppState`, `EvaluationBroker::submit`/`drain_pending`, `run_convergence_tick`. Pre-fix the regression test would FAIL at runtime: dispatching `Evaluation { reconciler: "job-lifecycle", target: "job/payments" }` causes BOTH `JobLifecycle::reconcile` AND `NoopHeartbeat::reconcile` to run against the JobLifecycle target because the body iterates `state.runtime.registered()` instead of looking up by name. The test's assertion shape (RCA §Regression test design — recommended weaker counter-based variant) — count `JobLifecycle::reconcile` invocations against `job/payments` (must be exactly 1 post-fix) and count `NoopHeartbeat::reconcile` invocations against the same target (must be exactly 0 post-fix). Marking the test `#[ignore]` in 01-01 keeps lefthook green without `--no-verify`; 01-02 removes the `#[ignore]` and the ignored→executed transition is the proof-of-fix.

Test placement: EXTEND existing file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` (default acceptance lane, NO `#![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]` gate per the entrypoint header at `tests/acceptance.rs`). Append the new `#[tokio::test] async fn eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler` next to the existing `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` test. Test fixture: `Arc<SimClock>`, `Arc<SimDriver(DriverType::Process)>`, `Arc<SimObservationStore>`, `Arc<LocalIntentStore>` on `tempfile::TempDir` — same shape as the existing test in the file. NO new mod registration needed (`mod runtime_convergence_loop;` already in `tests/acceptance.rs`). NO new wrapper module introduced — RCA §Regression test design — recommended variant says "counter-based variant is sufficient — assert `JobLifecycle::reconcile` ran against the job target while `NoopHeartbeat::reconcile` did NOT"; we instrument via SimDriver's recorded calls (sim adapter already counts) or by counting IntentStore `get` invocations through the existing sim adapter surface — no new wrapper module under `tests/`.

Counter approach for the RED assertion: `NoopHeartbeat::reconcile` is a pure function over the runtime that emits `vec![Action::Noop]` and performs zero observable I/O. The cleanest counter is to assert on broker-level invariants downstream of the dispatch: pre-fix, `NoopHeartbeat`'s self-re-enqueue path (now harmless after fix-noop-self-reenqueue) does NOT trigger because `Action::Noop` is filtered from `has_work`, but `NoopHeartbeat::hydrate` DOES execute (parsing the JobLifecycle target via `job_id_from_target` at `reconciler_runtime.rs:463-468` and reading from IntentStore + ObservationStore — wasted I/O per RCA §Observable consequence). The chosen counter shape: count IntentStore `get` invocations against `job/payments` per tick. Pre-fix the count is 2 (both reconcilers' `hydrate_desired` reads); post-fix the count is 1 (only `JobLifecycle::hydrate_desired` reads). Implementation: a thin counting wrapper over `LocalIntentStore` that delegates all methods and increments an `AtomicUsize` on `get`. The wrapper lives inline in the test file (NOT a new module under `tests/`). Acceptable alternative if the wrapper plumbing is heavier than the test's value warrants per RCA: use the broker's `dispatched` counter against a fixture where `NoopHeartbeat`'s incidental dispatch path produces an observable side effect — but the wrapper shape is preferred per the RCA's "strong shape is preferred" preference.

Out of scope (tracked separately, NOT a step in this roadmap). The RCA flagged that adding a DST invariant in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` asserting "for any drained `Evaluation { reconciler: R, target: T }`, exactly one reconciler — R — executes hydrate against T per tick" would close the §8 end-to-end gap (the broker collapse invariant exists; the dispatch invariant does not). That work is OUT OF SCOPE for this roadmap. Do not add it as a step. It will be tracked as a separate `fix-` feature.

Mutation gate compatibility (note for the GREEN step). The reconciler_runtime.rs edit produces a focused mutation surface around the `find()` predicate and the early-return path: cargo-mutants will generate (a) replacing `r.name() == reconciler_name` with `r.name() != reconciler_name` (would skip the named reconciler and run the wrong one) — killed by the new test's invocation-count assertions; (b) replacing the early-return `Ok(())` with a panic or unreachable — killed by an additional sub-assertion that an Evaluation referencing an unregistered reconciler logs+skips cleanly (this sub-assertion can be added defensively in the regression test or simply rely on the existing `cargo xtask dst` run not panicking); (c) replacing `reconciler_name.clone()` in the self-re-enqueue with a hardcoded other name — killed by the new test's `counters.queued == 0` invariant (the converged fixture should produce no re-enqueue regardless of name, but the chosen name is the one that landed in the broker key per the §8 storm-proofing intent — verified by inspecting broker counters after dispatch). The lib.rs caller edit produces a smaller surface (one new parameter pass-through); cargo-mutants will mutate the borrow shape but the new test exercises the call site end-to-end.

Mutation invocation per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing — uses `run_in_background: true` (the explicit foreground exception). Command: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs`. The `--features integration-tests` flag is required because the cascade test updates touch integration tests under `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/*.rs` (per RCA §Files affected — test code), and per testing.md §Workspace convention every member declares the flag. On macOS this MUST go through `cargo xtask lima run -- ...` per testing.md §Lima — the integration-tests-gated test surface is `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` and a degraded macOS-side mutation run silently understates kill rate. Kill rate gate: ≥80%. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome (per testing.md §post-mutation safety).

macOS-vs-Linux invocation hygiene per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running integration tests locally on macOS — Lima VM": (a) Default-lane invocations (the new regression test in 01-01, all `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` calls) run natively on macOS — the regression test is in `tests/acceptance/`, NOT behind `--features integration-tests`. (b) Integration-lane invocations (`cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`, mutation runs that pass `--features integration-tests`) MUST go through `cargo xtask lima run -- …` on macOS. The cascade test sites in `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/*.rs` ARE under integration-tests; running them executes through Lima. The macOS-side `--no-run` typecheck (`cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run`) is a necessary gate but not sufficient (per testing.md §Lima it catches type+wiring errors, NOT runtime/permission/cgroup invariants).

Standing user-rule references the crafter must obey: (1) `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations` — no shims, no deprecations, no flag-gated old paths. (2) `feedback_no_git_stash` — focused-subset commits use `git add <paths>` + `git restore --staged`, never `git stash`. (3) `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer` — clippy issues that surface during the work get fixed in-scope. (4) `feedback_no_rename_stubs` — no leftover stubs. (5) `feedback_delete_dont_gate` — no `#[cfg(test)]` annotations to silence dead-code warnings. (6) `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running tests — foreground, always" — every test invocation goes through `Bash` with `run_in_background: false` EXCEPT mutation testing. (7) `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running integration tests locally on macOS — Lima VM" — `--features integration-tests` invocations on macOS go through `cargo xtask lima run -- …`. (8) CLAUDE.md "Roadmap validator warnings" — non-blocking; do NOT trim AC specificity to satisfy them.

Roadmap-validator length warnings (`STEP_NAME_TOO_LONG`, `CRITERIA_TOO_LONG`, `DESCRIPTION_TOO_LONG`) are non-blocking per CLAUDE.md "Roadmap validator warnings" — Overdrive convention deliberately carries scenario-level specificity in ACs (test names, predicate shape, line numbers, parameter positions, gate predicates). Do NOT trim ACs to satisfy these warnings.

reviews.scope: RCA + Option A vs Option B trade analysis (Option A: change `run_convergence_tick` signature to take `reconciler_name: &ReconcilerName`, replace registry iteration with `reconcilers_iter().find(|r| r.name() == reconciler_name)`, log+skip on miss, pass `&eval.reconciler` from the caller. Option B: keep the registry-iterating fan-out and document it as intentional — REJECTED on grounds enumerated in the RCA: requires a new contract document explaining why the broker's key includes a field the dispatcher ignores, defensive target-shape handling in every reconciler that joins the registry, and a revised §8 invariant weaker than the whitepaper currently promises).
reviews.notes: User confirmed root cause (the dispatch path was authored for a single-reconciler era; `eval.reconciler` is dead code; the §8 storm-proofing invariant is asserted at the broker boundary only and there is no end-to-end test that submits N Evaluations across M reconcilers and asserts M reconcile calls total). User APPROVED Option A specifically over Option B on the architectural grounds enumerated in the RCA — "the trade is preserve a one-line, no-allocation `for` loop against preserve the storm-proofing invariant end-to-end; Option A wins on every axis." Single-cut migration shape confirmed: signature change + body rewrite + 5 cascade test updates land in one cohesive commit, no shims, no deprecations. Out of scope explicitly: DST invariant addition in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` asserting "for any drained Evaluation { reconciler: R, target: T }, exactly one reconciler — R — executes hydrate against T per tick" — tracked separately as a follow-up `fix-` feature, NOT a step in this roadmap. Reference RCA: `docs/feature/fix-eval-reconciler-discarded/deliver/bugfix-rca.md`.

phases.name: Dispatch only the reconciler named in `eval.reconciler` so the §8 storm-proofing invariant holds end-to-end through the broker→dispatcher path
phases.description: Two-step Outside-In TDD bugfix. Step 01-01 EXTENDS the existing acceptance test file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` (already registered through `tests/acceptance.rs`'s `mod acceptance { ... }` block from the prior `fix-noop-self-reenqueue` work) with one new `#[tokio::test]` named `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler`. The test builds an `AppState` with both reconcilers registered (`job-lifecycle` and `noop-heartbeat`), backed by a counting wrapper over `LocalIntentStore` that increments an `AtomicUsize` on `get(IntentKey::Job(...))` calls against `job/payments`. The fixture preloads one converged job, submits ONE `Evaluation { reconciler: ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle")?, target: TargetResource::new("job/payments")? }`, drains pending and dispatches via the same shape lib.rs uses (passing `&eval.reconciler` alongside `&eval.target` — which IS the post-fix call site shape). The test asserts: post-tick, the counter shows exactly 1 `get` against `job/payments` (only `JobLifecycle::hydrate_desired` ran), NOT 2 (which is the pre-fix value because both reconcilers' hydrate paths execute under the registry-iterating fan-out). Pre-fix this test would FAIL at runtime on the count==1 assertion; the test is committed marked `#[ignore = "RED scaffold for fix-eval-reconciler-discarded — un-ignore in the GREEN step"]` so lefthook's nextest-affected pre-commit pass stays green without `--no-verify`. Step 01-02 applies Option A in a single cohesive commit: (a) production edits in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` — change `run_convergence_tick` signature to add `reconciler_name: &ReconcilerName` (placement: after `state`, before `target`); replace the `for name in &registered` loop with `let Some(reconciler) = state.runtime.reconcilers_iter().find(|r| r.name() == reconciler_name) else { tracing::warn!(...); return Ok(()); };` and dedent the rest of the body out of the loop; self-re-enqueue at the end uses `reconciler_name.clone()` instead of `name.clone()`; revise the three doc-comment / body-comment blocks at `:174-176` (outer doc), `:189-194` (self-re-enqueue paragraph), and `:214-216` (body comment) so all three describe the lookup-by-name single-reconciler-dispatch contract. (b) caller edit in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:470-481` — pass `&eval.reconciler` alongside `&eval.target` into `run_convergence_tick`; add `reconciler = %eval.reconciler` to the `tracing::warn!` macro args. (c) mechanical signature updates at the 5 direct call sites listed in RCA §Files affected — test code: `tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` (both the existing `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` test AND the new `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler` test must construct a `ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle").expect(...)` or `noop-heartbeat` where the test is dispatching that one and pass it to `run_convergence_tick`); ALSO revise the wrong-contract comment at `:107-108` to describe the named-reconciler dispatch; ALSO remove the `#[ignore]` from `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler`; `tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_backoff.rs`; `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/submit_to_running.rs`; `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/stop_to_terminated.rs`; `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/crash_recovery.rs`. Single-cut migration: production edits + un-ignore + 5 cascade test updates land in ONE cohesive commit. No `#[deprecated]` overload, no shim function with feature flag, no two-phase rollout. The regression test transitions ignored → executed-and-passing within Step 01-02's commit. All existing acceptance, integration, and DST tests must remain GREEN. Quality gates per ACs: `cargo nextest run --workspace` green, `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` green, `cargo xtask dst` green, `cargo xtask dst-lint` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` typechecks on macOS.

steps.name: RED regression test — drained `Evaluation { reconciler, target }` must dispatch only the named reconciler against the target
steps.description: EXTEND the existing acceptance test file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` (already registered through `tests/acceptance.rs`'s `mod acceptance { ... }` block from `fix-noop-self-reenqueue`; do NOT create a new file; do NOT add a new `mod` declaration) with one new `#[tokio::test]` named `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler`. Per RCA §Regression test design — "extend the existing file rather than create a new one" — the file is the canonical landing point for runtime-loop regression tests. Test signature: `#[tokio::test] #[ignore = "RED scaffold for fix-eval-reconciler-discarded — un-ignore in the GREEN step"] async fn eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler() { ... }`. Body shape (per RCA §Regression test design — recommended counter-based variant): (1) Construct the same sim-adapter fixture pattern the existing `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` test in this file uses for its imports (`Arc<SimClock>`, `Arc<SimDriver(DriverType::Process)>`, `Arc<SimObservationStore::single_peer(NodeId::new("local")?, 0)>`, `LocalIntentStore::open(tmp.path().join("intent.redb"))?`). (2) Wrap `LocalIntentStore` in a thin counting struct defined inline in the test function (or as a private item in the file): `struct CountingIntentStore { inner: Arc<dyn IntentStore>, get_count_payments: Arc<AtomicUsize> }` implementing `IntentStore` — every method delegates to `inner` unchanged EXCEPT `get(key)` which checks if `key` is `IntentKey::Job(JobId::new("payments")?)` and increments `get_count_payments` before delegating. The wrapper lives in the test module — NOT a new module under `tests/`. (3) Build `AppState::new(counting_store, obs, runtime, driver)` (with `ReconcilerRuntime::new(...)` registering both `job-lifecycle` and `noop-heartbeat` per the canonical setup). (4) Preload IntentStore with one converged job (`Job { id: JobId::new("payments")?, replicas: 1, ... }` written through the inner store before wrapping — or via the wrapper, the test author chooses; setup writes do NOT count toward the test assertion because the counter only increments on `get`); preload observation with one Running alloc against that job (same helper pattern the existing test in this file uses). (5) Submit ONE Evaluation: `state.runtime.broker().submit(Evaluation { reconciler: ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle")?, target: TargetResource::new("job/payments")? });`. (6) Drain pending via `let pending = state.runtime.broker().drain_pending();`. (7) Dispatch each drained eval through the post-fix call shape: `for eval in pending { let _ = run_convergence_tick(&state, &eval.reconciler, &eval.target, now, tick_n, deadline).await; }` — the test is WRITTEN against the post-fix `run_convergence_tick(state, reconciler_name, target, ...)` signature, which is what makes it a RED scaffold: it WILL NOT COMPILE against current main until 01-02 lands the signature change. Per the project's RED-scaffold convention (`.claude/rules/testing.md` § "RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits") this is the correct shape — the compile failure IS the specification of work not yet done. NOTE: this means committing 01-01 requires `git commit --no-verify` because lefthook's `cargo check` will fail on the un-ignored compile error. The `#[ignore]` attribute does not protect from compile failures, only from runtime execution. Per testing.md § RED scaffolds: "Commit with `git commit --no-verify` and call it out explicitly in the commit message or user-facing summary. Intentionally-RED commits are one of the explicit exceptions to 'never skip hooks'." This is preferable to the alternative of writing the test against the pre-fix signature (which would silently keep working post-fix because the `eval.reconciler` would be silently dropped) — the scaffold makes the missing signature load-bearing. (8) Assertion (kills the bugged behaviour): `let count = counter.load(Ordering::SeqCst); assert_eq!(count, 1, "expected exactly one IntentStore::get against job/payments — JobLifecycle::hydrate_desired only — got {count} (pre-fix value 2 indicates fan-out across both reconcilers)");`. Pre-fix value: 2 (because the registry-iterating loop runs BOTH reconcilers' `hydrate_desired`, each parsing the JobLifecycle target via `job_id_from_target` at `reconciler_runtime.rs:463-468` and reading from IntentStore — RCA §Observable consequence). Post-fix value: 1 (only `JobLifecycle` runs because the named lookup finds it; `NoopHeartbeat` is never looked up because no `(noop-heartbeat, ...)` eval was submitted). (9) Optional defensive sub-assertion against the negative case: do NOT add (the RCA §Regression test design's strong shape calls for it but the recommendation in the spec is the weaker variant; keep the test focused on the count-==-1 invariant). The crafter MUST NOT add behavior beyond this single test function in this commit; this step exists exclusively to write the RED scaffold. No production-code edits in this step. Sub-task: NOTHING in `tests/acceptance.rs` needs editing — the `mod runtime_convergence_loop;` declaration already exists from `fix-noop-self-reenqueue`. Commit message: `test(reconciler): pin drained Evaluation must dispatch only the named reconciler (RED scaffold — see Step-ID: 01-01)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit goes through `git commit --no-verify` because the test is written against the post-fix signature (compile failure IS the specification of pending work) — the user-facing summary explicitly calls out the `--no-verify` and explains that 01-02's GREEN landing fixes the compile failure AND removes the `#[ignore]` in the same commit.
steps.criteria: Existing file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` extended with ONE new `#[tokio::test] #[ignore = "RED scaffold for fix-eval-reconciler-discarded — un-ignore in the GREEN step"] async fn eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler()` function (and no other tests). The body builds an `AppState` with both reconcilers registered, wraps `LocalIntentStore` in a `CountingIntentStore` defined inline (counter increments on `IntentStore::get` calls keyed against `JobId::new("payments")`), preloads one converged job, submits exactly one `Evaluation { reconciler: "job-lifecycle", target: "job/payments" }`, drains pending, dispatches each drained eval through `run_convergence_tick(&state, &eval.reconciler, &eval.target, now, tick_n, deadline)` (post-fix signature shape), and asserts `counter.load(Ordering::SeqCst) == 1`. The test is committed with `#[ignore]` attribute. NO new file under `tests/`; NO new `mod` declaration in `tests/acceptance.rs`; NO modification to the existing `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` test in this file. The new file does NOT carry `#![cfg(...)]` or `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]`. RED verification: against current main, `cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests` FAILS with a parameter-count or argument-mismatch error on the `run_convergence_tick(&state, &eval.reconciler, &eval.target, ...)` call site (current signature takes 5 args excluding state; the test passes 6) — this compile failure IS the RED scaffold per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits". Commit goes through `git commit --no-verify` (acknowledged exception per testing.md) with the user-facing summary calling out the `--no-verify` and the post-fix-signature shape. No production-code files touched (`git diff --name-only HEAD` shows only the modified `tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs`). No new feature flags introduced. Commit message: `test(reconciler): pin drained Evaluation must dispatch only the named reconciler (RED scaffold — see Step-ID: 01-01)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer.
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Defect (closure pin) — the new test directly demonstrates the user-observable bug: after one edge-triggered Evaluation against a converged AppState, the IntentStore receives 2 `get` calls against `job/payments` per tick (both `JobLifecycle::hydrate_desired` AND `NoopHeartbeat::hydrate_desired` execute) instead of the documented 1 call (only the named reconciler should run). The test pins the post-fix expected value (1) and asserts the precise causal invariant: named-reconciler dispatch implies that-reconciler-only hydrate execution.

RCA §Root cause closure — the test is written against the post-fix `run_convergence_tick(state, reconciler_name, target, now, tick_n, deadline)` signature; the compile failure under current main IS the proof that `eval.reconciler` is dead code in the dispatch path today and must become load-bearing.

RCA §Fix surface — Option A — the test pins the lookup-by-name dispatch contract: only `JobLifecycle` (the reconciler named in the eval) runs against the JobLifecycle target; `NoopHeartbeat` is never looked up because no `(noop-heartbeat, ...)` eval was submitted.

RCA §Risk closure — the test isolates the dispatch-routing defect: the converged-state fixture means `JobLifecycle::reconcile` returns `(Vec::new(), view.clone())` (no actions, no self-re-enqueue) and `NoopHeartbeat::reconcile` is never called post-fix. The test does not depend on action emission shape; it depends only on hydrate execution counts. Robust against future changes to `Action::Noop` filtering, `JobLifecycle` action emission, or the broker's collapse threshold.

RCA §Regression test design — the test is named `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler` per the RCA's specification; lives at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` (extends the existing file per the RCA's explicit instruction); uses the recommended weaker counter-based variant per the RCA's preferred shape ("counter verifying that only `job-lifecycle` reconcile was invoked"); fails against current code; passes after the GREEN step lands.

Test placement per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Layout": EXTENDS the existing file under `tests/acceptance/` already registered through the entrypoint's `mod acceptance { ... }` block. The acceptance suite stays in the default unit lane per the entrypoint header comment. NO `mod` declaration changes to `tests/acceptance.rs`.

RED verified at compile time per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits": pre-fix the test does not compile (`run_convergence_tick` arity/parameter mismatch); the `#[ignore]` attribute does not affect the compile gate; the commit goes through `git commit --no-verify` (acknowledged scaffold exception). The user-facing summary explicitly calls out the `--no-verify` and explains 01-02 fixes both the compile failure AND removes the `#[ignore]` in the same commit.

The crafter does NOT introduce additional test cases beyond `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler`, does NOT add helper modules, does NOT modify `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` (that's a 01-02 cascade edit), does NOT introduce a shared fixture file. The new test function and its inline `CountingIntentStore` wrapper are appended to the existing file.
No production-code edits (production fix lands in 01-02). No new feature flags introduced. The only file modified is `tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs`.

Commit message carries `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer. Pre-commit hooks SKIPPED via `git commit --no-verify` per the RED-scaffold exception (testing.md §RED scaffolds). The user-facing summary explicitly enumerates the `--no-verify` rationale (compile failure on post-fix signature is the specification of pending work) and confirms 01-02 will land both the production fix AND the un-ignore in one cohesive commit.
steps.tier: Tier 1 (DST/acceptance — default unit lane; sim traits only, no real I/O, runs on macOS and Linux uniformly).
quality_gates: RED scaffold compile-failure verified: `cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests` exits non-zero with a parameter-count/argument-mismatch on the `run_convergence_tick(&state, &eval.reconciler, &eval.target, ...)` call site. This is the proof-of-RED.
Other crates compile clean: `cargo check --workspace --exclude overdrive-control-plane --all-targets` exits 0.
Workspace-wide default lane stays green outside the affected crate: `cargo nextest run --workspace --exclude overdrive-control-plane` exits 0.
No production-code paths touched: `git diff --name-only HEAD` shows only the modified `tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs`.
No `#![cfg(...)]` attribute on the file beyond what's already there; no `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` introduced (the test uses sim adapters, platform-independent).
No new env-var / cwd / process-global mutation introduced — `#[serial_test::serial(env)]` is NOT needed; the test uses tempdir-scoped state.

Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit acknowledged through `git commit --no-verify` per the RED-scaffold exception (testing.md § "RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits"); user-facing summary explicitly calls out the `--no-verify` rationale (post-fix-signature compile failure is the proof of pending work) and confirms 01-02 lands both production fix AND un-ignore in one cohesive commit.

Single commit through `git commit --no-verify` (RED-scaffold exception per testing.md), with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer and the user-facing summary explicitly calling out the `--no-verify` rationale and confirming 01-02 closes the compile loop.
steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE EXECUTED — re-read RCA §Defect, §Observable consequence, §Fix surface — Option A, §Files affected (test code), §Regression test design. Audit existing fixtures: (a) the existing `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` test in `tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` for the canonical sim-adapter import surface and AppState construction shape (this is the file we're extending). (b) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs:207-285` for the current `run_convergence_tick` shape (signature, body, self-re-enqueue path) to confirm the post-fix call shape `(&state, &eval.reconciler, &eval.target, now, tick_n, deadline)`. (c) `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/intent_store.rs` for the `IntentStore` trait surface (to define the `CountingIntentStore` wrapper's delegating method bodies). (d) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:470-481` for the current caller shape (passes only `&eval.target` today; the post-fix shape adds `&eval.reconciler`). RED_ACCEPTANCE EXECUTED — write the test against the POST-FIX signature. Imports needed: in addition to the existing imports already in the file from `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue`: `std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering}`, `async_trait::async_trait` (for the `CountingIntentStore` impl). The `CountingIntentStore` struct is private to the test module: `struct CountingIntentStore { inner: Arc<dyn IntentStore>, get_count_payments: Arc<AtomicUsize> }` with `#[async_trait]` impl delegating all methods; the `get` impl matches against `IntentKey::Job(jid)` where `jid == &JobId::new("payments")?` and increments before delegating. NOTE: the exact `IntentKey` variant shape and the `IntentStore` trait method signatures must be confirmed against current core; if `IntentKey` does not destructure cleanly (e.g. it's an opaque newtype), an alternative is to count get-by-bytes via `IntentKey::as_bytes()` substring match — the crafter chooses the cleanest shape that compiles. RED_UNIT SKIPPED — the test exercises only the runtime's existing public surface (post-fix); no internal helper introduced beyond `CountingIntentStore`. GREEN SKIPPED — production fix lands in Step 01-02. COMMIT EXECUTED — `git commit --no-verify` (acknowledged RED-scaffold exception per testing.md §RED scaffolds); commit body explains: (1) this is a RED scaffold; (2) the test is written against the POST-FIX signature so it does not compile against current main; (3) per testing.md §RED scaffolds the correct shape is `git commit --no-verify` rather than weakening the assertion to a runtime-failing variant against the pre-fix signature (which would silently keep passing post-fix); (4) Step 01-02 lands the production fix, removes the `#[ignore]`, AND the test transitions from un-compiled-and-ignored to compiled-and-passing in one commit. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase` — do NOT edit `execution-log.json` directly. CRITICAL crafter callouts: (1) The `tests/acceptance.rs` mod-declaration already includes `mod runtime_convergence_loop;` from `fix-noop-self-reenqueue` — do NOT add another. (2) The test fixture preloads one job (`JobId::new("payments")?`) via the inner store BEFORE wrapping it in `CountingIntentStore` (or via the wrapper if cleaner — setup writes don't increment the counter because the counter only fires on `get`, not `put`). (3) The drain-and-dispatch loop is one tick deep — the test's invariant (count==1 after a single dispatch of a single Evaluation) is per-tick, not bounded across multiple ticks. (4) The `CountingIntentStore` wrapper's only purpose is the count — every method is a thin delegation. Do NOT add transformation, caching, or any other behaviour. (5) Pre-fix value verified by inspection of `reconciler_runtime.rs:217-218` (`for name in &registered { ... }`) and `:236-237` (where each iteration's `hydrate_desired` reads from IntentStore) — both reconcilers' `hydrate_desired` paths execute per drained eval, so the counter increments twice (once for `JobLifecycle`, once for `NoopHeartbeat`). Post-fix value 1 verified by inspection of the RCA §Code shape — the `find()` lookup runs exactly one reconciler's `hydrate_desired`.

steps.name: GREEN minimal fix — Option A: dispatch only the named reconciler from each drained Evaluation, plus 5 cascade test signature updates and un-ignore
steps.description: Apply the RCA §Fix surface — Option A in a single cohesive commit. The diff comprises THREE production-code edit clusters plus FIVE cascade test-site updates plus ONE un-ignore. PRODUCTION EDITS — `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs`: (1) Change `run_convergence_tick` signature to add `reconciler_name: &ReconcilerName` parameter — placement after `state`, before `target`. New signature: `pub async fn run_convergence_tick(state: &AppState, reconciler_name: &ReconcilerName, target: &TargetResource, now: Instant, tick_n: u64, deadline: Instant) -> Result<(), ConvergenceError>`. (2) Replace the `let registered = state.runtime.registered(); for name in &registered { ... }` loop body (currently `:217-285`) with a single name-based lookup: `let Some(reconciler) = state.runtime.reconcilers_iter().find(|r| r.name() == reconciler_name) else { tracing::warn!(target: "overdrive::reconciler", reconciler = %reconciler_name, target = %target.as_str(), "convergence tick: reconciler not registered; skipping"); return Ok(()); };` and dedent the rest of the body (the `hydrate_desired`, `hydrate_actual`, `LibsqlHandle::default_phase1`, `reconciler.hydrate(target, &db).await`, `cached_view_or_default`, `reconciler.reconcile(...)`, `store_cached_view`, `has_work`, `action_shim::dispatch`, self-re-enqueue path) out of the for-loop. (3) Self-re-enqueue at the end uses `reconciler_name.clone()` instead of `name.clone()` — the broker submission becomes `state.runtime.broker().submit(Evaluation { reconciler: reconciler_name.clone(), target: target.clone() });`. (4) Revise the THREE doc-comment / body-comment blocks so all three describe the lookup-by-name single-reconciler-dispatch contract: `:174-176` outer rustdoc comment (currently says "the registered `JobLifecycle` reconciler (Phase 1 single-target shape)" — revise to: "Drive ONE convergence tick against `target` for the reconciler named in `reconciler_name`. Looked up via `reconcilers_iter().find(...)`; if not registered, log a warning and return Ok cleanly (the reconciler may have been deregistered between submit and drain)."), `:189-194` self-re-enqueue paragraph (currently describes the registry-iteration semantics — revise to describe single-reconciler dispatch and the §8 storm-proofing intent: "Re-enqueue under the same `(reconciler_name, target)` key the inbound Evaluation carried — the broker collapses redundant submits at the same key per ADR-0013 §8 / whitepaper §18."), `:214-216` body comment (currently `// Phase 1: drive only the JobLifecycle reconciler against the target...` — revise to: "// Look up the named reconciler from the registered set. The Evaluation's `reconciler` field is the broker's key half and is now the dispatch target. Each drained Evaluation runs exactly one reconciler — the one it names."). CALLER EDIT — `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:470-481`: pass `&eval.reconciler` alongside `&eval.target` into `run_convergence_tick`. Update the `tracing::warn!` macro args at `:474-479` to add `reconciler = %eval.reconciler` to the structured fields (preserve the existing `?e` and `target_name = %eval.target.as_str()`). MECHANICAL CASCADE TEST UPDATES (5 files) — at every direct `run_convergence_tick(&state, &target, now, tick_n, deadline).await` call site, insert `&reconciler_name` (a `ReconcilerName` constructed via `ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle").expect("job-lifecycle reconciler name")` for tests dispatching JobLifecycle convergence, or `"noop-heartbeat"` where the test is dispatching that one — none of the cascade tests today dispatch noop-heartbeat, but the rule is: pass the reconciler the test is exercising) as the second argument: (a) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` — both the existing `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` test AND the new `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler` test must construct a `ReconcilerName` and pass it. ALSO revise the wrong-contract comment at `:107-108` (currently says "the convergence-tick loop in lib.rs::run_server_with_obs_and_driver drains the broker per tick and runs every registered reconciler against each drained target") to: "the convergence-tick loop in lib.rs::run_server_with_obs_and_driver drains the broker per tick and dispatches each drained Evaluation to exactly the reconciler named in `eval.reconciler` against the eval's target — N entries collapse to N dispatches per distinct `(reconciler, target)` key, per whitepaper §18 / ADR-0013 §8.". ALSO remove the `#[ignore = "RED scaffold for fix-eval-reconciler-discarded — un-ignore in the GREEN step"]` attribute from `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler`. (b) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_backoff.rs` — pass the JobLifecycle reconciler name at every direct `run_convergence_tick` call site. (c) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/submit_to_running.rs` (RCA cites `:79-87`) — same. (d) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/stop_to_terminated.rs` — same. (e) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/crash_recovery.rs` — same. Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: production edits + un-ignore + 5 cascade test updates land in ONE cohesive commit. No `#[deprecated]` overload, no shadow function with feature flag, no two-phase rollout. Commit message: `fix(reconciler): dispatch only the named reconciler per drained evaluation` with body citing the RCA path (`docs/feature/fix-eval-reconciler-discarded/deliver/bugfix-rca.md`) and `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer. Normal pre-commit hooks apply (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: Production changes (single cohesive commit) — exactly the edit clusters enumerated in the description: (1) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` `run_convergence_tick` signature now reads `pub async fn run_convergence_tick(state: &AppState, reconciler_name: &ReconcilerName, target: &TargetResource, now: Instant, tick_n: u64, deadline: Instant) -> Result<(), ConvergenceError>` (verified by `git grep -n 'pub async fn run_convergence_tick' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs`). (2) The body uses `state.runtime.reconcilers_iter().find(|r| r.name() == reconciler_name)` for the named lookup with an `else { tracing::warn!(...); return Ok(()); }` early-return on miss; the rest of the body (`hydrate_desired`, `hydrate_actual`, `LibsqlHandle::default_phase1`, `reconciler.hydrate(target, &db).await`, `cached_view_or_default`, `reconciler.reconcile(...)`, `store_cached_view`, `has_work`, `action_shim::dispatch`, self-re-enqueue path) is dedented out of any for-loop (verified by `git grep -n 'for name in &registered' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` returning ZERO matches). (3) Self-re-enqueue uses `reconciler_name.clone()` (not `name.clone()`); the broker submission shape is `state.runtime.broker().submit(Evaluation { reconciler: reconciler_name.clone(), target: target.clone() })`. (4) Three doc/body-comment blocks (`:174-176` outer rustdoc, `:189-194` self-re-enqueue paragraph, `:214-216` body comment) revised to describe the lookup-by-name single-reconciler-dispatch contract (verified by reading the diff — comments must mention "the reconciler named in `reconciler_name`" or equivalent and remove any "every registered reconciler" or "Phase 1 single-target shape" language). (5) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:470-481` passes `&eval.reconciler` alongside `&eval.target`; the `tracing::warn!` envelope at `:474-479` includes `reconciler = %eval.reconciler` in its structured fields (verified by `git grep -n 'reconciler = %eval.reconciler' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs`). (6) Five cascade test files updated at every `run_convergence_tick` call site to pass the reconciler name as the second argument; the wrong-contract comment in `runtime_convergence_loop.rs:107-108` is revised to describe the named-reconciler dispatch; the `#[ignore]` is removed from `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler`. Test verification: (1) Step 01-01's regression test transitions un-compiled-and-ignored → compiled-and-passing within this commit (the signature change makes the test compile; the `#[ignore]` removal makes it execute; the `find()`-based lookup makes the assertion `count == 1` pass). (2) `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0 with `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler` reported as PASS (not ignored). (3) All existing acceptance and integration tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0 (default lane); `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` exits 0 (when run on Linux directly or on macOS via `cargo xtask lima run -- ...`); `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` typechecks on macOS. (4) `cargo xtask dst` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 (the new code uses only existing `core`-class types — `ReconcilerName`, `TargetResource`, the `reconcilers_iter()` accessor — no new banned-call surface in the wiring crate). (5) `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on `overdrive-control-plane`. (6) Mutation gate (post-GREEN, run with `run_in_background: true` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing exception, on macOS via `cargo xtask lima run -- ...` per testing.md §Lima): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs` reports kill rate ≥80%. The new test's invocation-count assertion kills the canonical predicate mutations: (a) flipping `r.name() == reconciler_name` to `r.name() != reconciler_name` killed by the new test (would skip JobLifecycle, count==0 instead of expected 1, AND would dispatch NoopHeartbeat instead, count==1 against noop's hydrate path — either way the assertion fails); (b) replacing the early-return `Ok(())` with a panic killed by the workspace's `cargo xtask dst` run (DST scenarios that submit unknown reconciler names would panic instead of skipping); (c) replacing `reconciler_name.clone()` in the self-re-enqueue with a different `ReconcilerName` literal killed by the integration tests in `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/*.rs` which exercise the multi-tick convergence loop — a wrong re-enqueue name would cause the next-tick lookup to miss and the test's expected state transition would not occur. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. Single-cut migration: `git grep -E '#\[deprecated|#\[allow\(deprecated\]|TODO.*remove|backwards-compat' crates/overdrive-control-plane/` returns no matches in the diff; no shim function, no feature-flagged old path, no parallel `run_convergence_tick_v1`/`run_convergence_tick_v2` shapes. Commit message: `fix(reconciler): dispatch only the named reconciler per drained evaluation` with body citing `docs/feature/fix-eval-reconciler-discarded/deliver/bugfix-rca.md` and `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`). Post-GREEN smoke check: `git diff --stat HEAD~1` shows the expected breadth — `reconciler_runtime.rs` (signature/body/comment cluster), `lib.rs` (caller cluster), and 5 test files (signature pass-through cluster), nothing else.
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Defect (closure) — `run_convergence_tick` no longer iterates `state.runtime.registered()`. The function takes `reconciler_name: &ReconcilerName` as the second parameter and looks up exactly one reconciler via `reconcilers_iter().find(|r| r.name() == reconciler_name)`. `eval.reconciler` is now load-bearing dispatch input, not dead code.

RCA §Root cause closure — the dispatch path's contract is now consistent with the broker's `(reconciler, target)` key. The §8 storm-proofing invariant (per ADR-0013 §8 / whitepaper §18) holds end-to-end: N redundant submits collapse to 1 broker entry per distinct key, and 1 broker entry produces exactly 1 reconcile call (against the named reconciler), not M reconcile calls (one per registered reconciler).

RCA §Fix surface — Option A — the implementation matches the RCA's specified code shape exactly: signature `(state, reconciler_name, target, now, tick_n, deadline)`, body `let Some(reconciler) = state.runtime.reconcilers_iter().find(|r| r.name() == reconciler_name) else { tracing::warn!(...); return Ok(()); };`, self-re-enqueue uses `reconciler_name.clone()`. The three doc/body-comment blocks (`:174-176`, `:189-194`, `:214-216`) are revised to describe the lookup-by-name contract — no "every registered reconciler" or "Phase 1 single-target shape" language remains.

Step 01-01's regression test transitions un-compiled-and-ignored → compiled-and-passing within this commit. The signature change makes the test compile; the `#[ignore]` removal makes it execute; the `find()`-based lookup makes the assertion `count == 1` pass (only `JobLifecycle::hydrate_desired` runs against `job/payments`; `NoopHeartbeat::hydrate_desired` is never invoked because no `(noop-heartbeat, ...)` eval was submitted).

RCA §Files affected — test code — all 5 cascade test files updated: `tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` (both tests + comment + un-ignore), `tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_backoff.rs`, `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/submit_to_running.rs`, `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/stop_to_terminated.rs`, `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/crash_recovery.rs`. Every direct `run_convergence_tick` call site in these files passes a `&ReconcilerName` as the second argument. The wrong-contract comment in `runtime_convergence_loop.rs:107-108` is revised to describe the named-reconciler dispatch.

All existing tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` typechecks on macOS; `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` exits 0 on Linux (or on macOS via `cargo xtask lima run -- ...`).

Doctest coverage preserved: any `///` rustdoc example on `run_convergence_tick` is updated to show the post-fix signature; if no doctest exists today (the function is internal-ish), no new doctest is required.

Reconciler purity preserved per `.claude/rules/development.md` §Reconciler I/O — `run_convergence_tick` continues to construct `TickContext { now, tick, deadline }` from the runtime-injected `Clock`; the `find()` lookup is a pure function over the registered set; the early-return `Ok(())` on miss preserves the existing error-shape contract; no new I/O introduced. `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0.

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: production edits + un-ignore + 5 cascade test updates in one cohesive commit. No `#[deprecated]` overload, no `run_convergence_tick_with_name` shadow function, no feature-flagged old path. `git grep -E '#\[deprecated|TODO.*remove|backwards-compat' crates/overdrive-control-plane/` returns no matches in the diff.

`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green on `overdrive-control-plane`. The `find(|r| r.name() == reconciler_name)` shape passes clippy without any `#[allow(...)]` annotation. Any clippy issues that surface during the work are fixed in-scope per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer`.

If any mutation survives, add a concrete test case that disambiguates it — NOT weaken existing assertions, NOT relax the lookup-by-name contract.

steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE EXECUTED — re-read RCA §Fix surface — Option A and §Why Option B was rejected. Confirm current line numbers in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` (the RCA cites `:207-285` for the function body, `:174-176`/`:189-194`/`:214-216` for the doc/body comments, `:217-218` for the `for name in &registered` loop, `:264-275` for the self-re-enqueue path). Locate by symbol name and grep, not by absolute line number — the file may have drifted slightly. Confirm the `reconcilers_iter()` accessor exists on `ReconcilerRuntime` (search `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` for `pub fn reconcilers_iter`); if it does NOT exist today and only `registered()` does, the implementation either (a) adds `reconcilers_iter()` as a thin convenience method that returns `impl Iterator<Item = &dyn AnyReconciler>` over the registry's stored entries, or (b) uses the existing `registered()` accessor's return shape combined with a per-name lookup helper. The crafter chooses the cleanest shape that compiles; adding a tiny accessor on `ReconcilerRuntime` is the recommended option per the RCA's code-shape sketch. RED_ACCEPTANCE EXECUTED — Step 01-01 already wrote the regression test (currently uncompiled-and-ignored). Verify it currently does not compile against current main: `cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests` should fail with an arity/parameter-mismatch on the test's `run_convergence_tick(&state, &eval.reconciler, &eval.target, ...)` call site. RED_UNIT SKIPPED — no new internal helper introduced beyond optionally adding `reconcilers_iter()` to `ReconcilerRuntime`. GREEN EXECUTED — implement in this order: (1) If needed, add `pub fn reconcilers_iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &dyn AnyReconciler> + '_ { self.registered_inner().iter().map(|e| e.as_ref()) }` (or equivalent shape over whatever the current registry storage is) on `ReconcilerRuntime`. (2) Edit `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` `run_convergence_tick` signature: insert `reconciler_name: &ReconcilerName` as the second parameter (after `state`, before `target`). (3) Replace the body's `let registered = state.runtime.registered(); for name in &registered { ... }` block (currently `:217-285`) with: at the top of the function, a `let Some(reconciler) = state.runtime.reconcilers_iter().find(|r| r.name() == reconciler_name) else { tracing::warn!(target: "overdrive::reconciler", reconciler = %reconciler_name, target = %target.as_str(), "convergence tick: reconciler not registered; skipping"); return Ok(()); };`; immediately after, dedent the rest of the original loop body (the `hydrate_desired`, `hydrate_actual`, `LibsqlHandle::default_phase1`, `reconciler.hydrate(target, &db).await`, `cached_view_or_default`, `reconciler.reconcile(...)`, `store_cached_view`, `has_work`, `action_shim::dispatch`, self-re-enqueue path) out of the for-loop. Update the self-re-enqueue's broker submission to use `reconciler_name.clone()` instead of `name.clone()` (i.e., `state.runtime.broker().submit(Evaluation { reconciler: reconciler_name.clone(), target: target.clone() })`). (4) Revise the THREE doc/body-comment blocks. Outer rustdoc `:174-176`: replace "the registered `JobLifecycle` reconciler (Phase 1 single-target shape)" with prose describing the lookup-by-name contract — "Drive ONE convergence tick against `target` for the reconciler named in `reconciler_name`. The reconciler is looked up via `reconcilers_iter().find(...)`; if not registered, the function logs a structured warning and returns Ok cleanly (the reconciler may have been deregistered between submit and drain — Phase 2+ concern, defensively handled)." Self-re-enqueue paragraph `:189-194`: revise to describe single-reconciler dispatch and §8 storm-proofing — "Re-enqueue under the same `(reconciler_name, target)` key the inbound Evaluation carried — the broker collapses redundant submits at the same key per ADR-0013 §8 / whitepaper §18." Body comment `:214-216`: replace the "Phase 1: drive only the JobLifecycle reconciler..." comment with: "// Look up the named reconciler from the registered set. The Evaluation's `reconciler` field is the broker's key half and is now the dispatch target. Each drained Evaluation runs exactly one reconciler — the one it names." (5) Edit `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:470-481`: change the `run_convergence_tick(&state, &eval.target, now, tick_n, deadline)` call to `run_convergence_tick(&state, &eval.reconciler, &eval.target, now, tick_n, deadline)`. Update the `tracing::warn!` envelope at `:474-479` to add `reconciler = %eval.reconciler` to the structured fields (preserve `?e` and `target_name = %eval.target.as_str()`). (6) Run `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` — production code should now compile clean. (7) Cascade test updates — at every direct `run_convergence_tick(&state, &target, ...)` call site in the 5 listed test files, insert a `&ReconcilerName` constructed via `ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle").expect("job-lifecycle reconciler name")` (the cleanest shape is a local `let` binding at the top of the test or fixture-helper that constructs it once and reuses it). Files: `tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs` — both tests; `tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_backoff.rs`; `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/submit_to_running.rs`; `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/stop_to_terminated.rs`; `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/crash_recovery.rs`. (8) In `runtime_convergence_loop.rs`, also revise the wrong-contract comment at `:107-108` to describe the named-reconciler dispatch (per RCA §Files affected — test code). (9) In `runtime_convergence_loop.rs`, also remove the `#[ignore = "RED scaffold for fix-eval-reconciler-discarded — un-ignore in the GREEN step"]` attribute from `eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler`. (10) Run `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -E 'test(eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler)'` — the test should pass; the count assertion reports `1`. (11) Run `cargo nextest run --workspace` — full default lane stays green; the existing `noop_heartbeat_against_converged_target_does_not_re_enqueue` test continues to pass with its updated call site. (12) Run `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` (on Linux directly; on macOS via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests`) — green; the 3 cascade integration tests under `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/*.rs` continue to pass with their updated call sites. (13) Run `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` — green. (14) Run `cargo xtask dst` — green. (15) Run `cargo xtask dst-lint` — green. (16) Run `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` on `overdrive-control-plane` — green; fix any clippy issues in-scope per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer`. (17) Run `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck) — green. COMMIT EXECUTED — `git commit` (no `--no-verify`); commit body explicitly enumerates the production edits + cascade test updates + un-ignore; cites the RCA path. Single cohesive commit. POST-GREEN MUTATION RUN: per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing exception, mutation runs use `run_in_background: true`. On macOS, the invocation MUST go through `cargo xtask lima run --` per testing.md §Lima because `--features integration-tests` is in the command. Invoke `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs`. Wait for completion via the shell ID; parse `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` for the kill rate. Expected mutations to be killed (3 distinct classes per RCA notes): (a) flipping `r.name() == reconciler_name` to `r.name() != reconciler_name` killed by the new regression test's `count == 1` assertion (would skip JobLifecycle, count==0 instead); (b) replacing the early-return `Ok(())` with a panic killed by the existing DST suite (`cargo xtask dst`) — DST scenarios touch the function repeatedly and would hit the panic; (c) replacing `reconciler_name.clone()` in the self-re-enqueue with a different `ReconcilerName` literal killed by the 3 integration tests under `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/*.rs` which exercise multi-tick convergence. On any surviving mutation, add a concrete test case that disambiguates it AND re-run mutation — NOT weaken existing assertions, NOT relax the lookup-by-name contract. Always run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase`. Post-commit smoke check: re-run `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` (via Lima on macOS), `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane`, `cargo xtask dst`, and `cargo xtask dst-lint` one more time to confirm zero regressions; this is the input to Phase 3.5 post-merge integration gate. CRITICAL crafter callouts: (1) The new `ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle")` construction at every test call site should ideally be lifted to a single helper or local-const in each file rather than repeated per call site — but DO NOT over-engineer this; if a test has 1-2 call sites, inline construction is fine. (2) If the existing `runtime_convergence_loop.rs:107-108` comment cites different reasoning today than the RCA quoted, revise to the post-fix language regardless — the RCA's prescribed comment shape is the load-bearing artifact. (3) The integration tests' `run_convergence_tick` call sites today are at `submit_to_running.rs:79-87` per RCA §Files affected; the actual line numbers may have drifted — locate by grep, not by line number. (4) The `tracing::warn!` macro update in `lib.rs` requires `eval.reconciler` to implement `Display` (or `Debug` via `?`); `ReconcilerName` should already implement `Display` per overdrive-core convention (verify by grepping the trait surface) — if it doesn't, use `?eval.reconciler` instead of `%eval.reconciler` in the tracing args. (5) macOS-vs-Linux: every `cargo nextest run --features integration-tests` invocation on macOS during this step runs through `cargo xtask lima run -- ...`; the macOS `--no-run` typecheck pass is a necessary but not sufficient gate (testing.md §Lima).

validation.notes: Roadmap quality gate (6 checks) PASS: (1) AC coupling — refs only public API surface (`run_convergence_tick` signature, `Evaluation { reconciler, target }` fields, `EvaluationBroker::submit`/`drain_pending`/`counters` accessors, `ReconcilerName::new`, `IntentStore::get`); no references to private internal state. (2) Decomposition ratio 2 steps / 2 production files = 1.0, well under 2.5. (3) Two distinct step shapes: 01-01 RED scaffold (regression test only, no production code, `git commit --no-verify` per testing.md §RED scaffolds because the test is written against the post-fix signature); 01-02 GREEN minimal fix (signature change + body rewrite + caller update + 5 cascade test signature updates + un-ignore in one cohesive commit per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`). (4) Both steps have `files_to_modify` with absolute paths (01-01: 1 file; 01-02: 7 files — 2 production + 5 test). (5) Step IDs match `^\d{2}-\d{2}$` (01-01, 01-02). (6) RCA linkage — both steps trace to RCA sections by name: 01-01 to §Defect, §Observable consequence, §Fix surface — Option A (the test pins the post-fix contract), §Risk (test isolates the dispatch-routing defect from action-emission concerns), §Regression test design (extends the existing file, recommended counter-based variant); 01-02 to §Fix surface — Option A (signature, body, comments), §Files affected — production code (2 files), §Files affected — test code (5 cascade files), §Why Option A over Option B (storm-proofing intent restored, `eval.reconciler` no longer dead code, Phase 2+ correctness without further changes), §Risk (5 cascade test files identified explicitly, all mechanical signature updates). Phase 1d reviewer (nw-acceptance-designer-reviewer) skipped: this is a /nw-bugfix flow with no DISTILL artifact to verify scenario coverage against. The user-APPROVED RCA at `docs/feature/fix-eval-reconciler-discarded/deliver/bugfix-rca.md` is the load-bearing specification. Scope-discipline guardrails flagged in roadmap notes for crafter visibility: (a) the DST invariant addition in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` (the reviewer's hint that would close the §8 end-to-end gap by asserting "for any drained Evaluation { reconciler: R, target: T }, exactly one reconciler — R — executes hydrate against T per tick") is OUT OF SCOPE for this roadmap and tracked separately as a follow-up `fix-` feature; (b) NO new wrapper module under `tests/` — the `CountingIntentStore` for the regression test lives inline in `tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs`; (c) the 5 cascade test signature updates are mechanical pass-throughs — no semantic change to test intent; (d) the `--features integration-tests` flag is required on the mutation invocation because the cascade test surface includes `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/*.rs`; on macOS this MUST go through `cargo xtask lima run -- ...` per testing.md §Lima. Proceeding to Phase 2 execution.
